skill.name: nature-response
skill.description: Draft, audit, or revise point-by-point reviewer response letters for Nature-family manuscript revisions. Use when the user provides reviewer comments, editor decision letters, revision notes, response drafts, or asks how to respond to major/minor revision requests, rebuttal letters, response to reviewers, peer-review reports.
skill.summary: Academic peer-review response letter writer for Nature-family journals and general scientific publishing.
skill.author: Community contribution, refactored into static/dynamic layers

security_audit.summary: All 416 static analysis findings are false positives. The skill consists entirely of Markdown documentation files for academic peer-review response letter drafting. No executable code, scripts, or shell commands exist. 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are Markdown inline code formatting, not actual Ruby/shell commands. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are academic content discussing research methods. No real security risks were identified.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Execution Patterns
low_findings.description: 341 locations flagged for 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' are Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., `code`), not actual shell command execution. The skill contains no executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations are Markdown files containing academic documentation. Backticks are standard Markdown syntax for inline code spans, not Ruby/shell execution constructs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Approximately 69 locations flagged for 'weak cryptographic algorithm' are academic content discussing scientific methods, not actual cryptography. No cryptographic operations are performed by this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is an academic writing assistant with no cryptographic functionality. Flagged content is academic discussion of research methodology.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System/Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Several LOW severity flags for 'system reconnaissance' and 'network reconnaissance' are academic content discussing peer review methodology, not actual reconnaissance activity. Hardcoded URLs in source-basis.md are academic reference citations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are academic journal references (Nature, Science, etc.), not malicious endpoints. No network requests are made by the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: README.md flagged for high entropy (6.60 bits) due to varied content density in academic documentation. Normal for comprehensive Markdown documentation with code blocks, lists, and prose.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High entropy in Markdown files is expected due to mixed content types (headers, lists, code blocks, prose). No obfuscation or encryption present.

content.value_statement: Academic researchers struggle to write point-by-point response letters to reviewer comments under deadline pressure. This skill provides structured templates, tone guidance, and conflict resolution strategies for Nature-family and general scientific journal revisions.
actual_capabilities: Draft point-by-point responses to reviewer comments for major and minor revisions
Generate professional rebuttal letters with appropriate academic tone and stance
Handle conflicting reviewer recommendations with structured conflict resolution
Support Chinese-language academic correspondence (审稿回复, 修回信)
Provide QA checklist for response letter completeness and professionalism
Classify reviewer comments by type (major concerns, minor suggestions, clarifications)
limitations: Cannot guarantee journal acceptance or reviewer satisfaction
Does not perform actual experiments or generate scientific data
Response quality depends on the clarity of reviewer comments provided
Limited to text-based drafting; does not handle supplementary file preparation

use_cases.title: Major Revision Response
use_cases.description: Draft a comprehensive response letter addressing all major reviewer concerns for a Nature-family manuscript revision request.
use_cases.target_user: PhD students and postdocs facing major revision deadlines

use_cases.title: Minor Revision Polish
use_cases.description: Quickly generate concise responses to minor reviewer comments and clarification requests.
use_cases.target_user: Researchers with minor revision requests needing efficient turnaround

use_cases.title: Chinese Academic Correspondence
use_cases.description: Draft response letters in Chinese for domestic Chinese journals or bilingual correspondence with international reviewers.
use_cases.target_user: Chinese-speaking researchers submitting to Chinese or international journals

prompt_templates.title: Draft Major Revision Response
prompt_templates.prompt: Here are the reviewer comments for my manuscript: [paste comments]. Please draft a point-by-point response letter addressing each concern. The manuscript is for [journal name] and the decision was major revision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a major revision response from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Revise Existing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Please review and improve this response letter draft: [paste draft]. Check for tone, completeness, and professional academic style. Identify any reviewer comments I may have missed.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Conflicting Reviews
prompt_templates.prompt: Reviewer 1 suggests [X] but Reviewer 2 suggests [Y]. How should I handle this conflict in my response letter? Please draft a diplomatic response that addresses both perspectives.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Response Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Please audit my response letter against the original reviewer comments: [paste reviewer comments] and [paste response letter]. Flag any unaddressed concerns and suggest improvements.

output_examples.input: Reviewer 2: 'The sample size is too small to draw meaningful conclusions. Please increase to at least n=100 or provide statistical justification.'
output_examples.output: Response to Reviewer 2: We thank the reviewer for raising this important methodological point. Upon reflection, we agree that the original sample size (n=45) may limit statistical power. We have now expanded our cohort to n=112 patients recruited from three additional medical centers. The revised analysis (now in Supplementary Table S3) shows consistent results with improved statistical power (1-β = 0.92). We have added a detailed power analysis in the Methods section (page 12, lines 245-260).

output_examples.input: Reviewer 1: 'The discussion section is too long and should be condensed.' Reviewer 3: 'I would like to see more discussion of the limitations.'
output_examples.output: Response to Reviewers 1 and 3: We appreciate both reviewers' feedback on the discussion section. We have streamlined the overall discussion by removing redundant content (reduced from 2,800 to 1,900 words) while adding a dedicated 'Limitations and Future Directions' subsection (paragraph 4, page 18) that addresses Reviewer 3's concern. This balanced approach maintains conciseness while ensuring thorough treatment of methodological boundaries.

best_practices: Always respond to every single reviewer comment, even if briefly, to demonstrate thoroughness
Maintain a respectful and grateful tone throughout, even when disagreeing with reviewers
Quote the reviewer's exact words before your response for clarity and easy reference
Include specific page numbers, figure references, and line numbers when describing changes made
Use a consistent format (numbered responses matching reviewer comments) for professional presentation
anti_patterns: Do not copy-paste the same generic response for multiple comments without addressing each specifically
Avoid defensive or confrontational language when disagreeing with reviewer assessments
Do not ignore or skip minor comments, as this signals lack of attention to detail
Avoid making promises about future work that you cannot or will not complete

faq.question: What types of manuscripts does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Nature-family journals but works for any scientific journal requiring point-by-point response letters, including Cell, Science, PLOS, and domain-specific journals.

faq.question: Does this skill support Chinese-language responses?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes dedicated guidance for Chinese academic correspondence (审稿回复, 修回信, 回复审稿人) and can draft bilingual responses when needed.

faq.question: Can this skill help with the actual manuscript revision, not just the response letter?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on the response letter itself. For manuscript content revisions, you would need a separate editing workflow, though the response letter guidance can inform what changes to make.

faq.question: How does the skill handle conflicting reviewer recommendations?
faq.answer: The skill includes specific strategies for diplomatically addressing contradictory suggestions, typically by explaining your reasoning, proposing a compromise, or asking the editor for guidance.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for early-career researchers?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is particularly valuable for PhD students and postdocs who may be writing their first response letters. The structured templates and tone guidance help ensure professional quality.

faq.question: Does the skill guarantee acceptance or positive reviewer outcomes?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps you write a professional, complete response letter, but cannot guarantee journal acceptance. The quality of your research and revisions remains the primary factor.
